findings.evidence: Its sole use is `min_mul_prod_norm_sq_le_add hhalf.le hdef.le U` inside the private isCoercive_energyIntegrand_of_bounds; the rubric requires implementation helpers be private.
findings.fix: Mark it `private` (the other estimate helpers in this file are private); expose it only when a consumer requires it.
findings.issue: min_mul_prod_norm_sq_le_add is public and listed as a Main declaration, but it is a generic product-norm inequality used only once internally (in isCoercive_energyIntegrand_of_bounds); no named downstream target needs it.

findings.evidence: Diff: private `@[grind =>] private lemma isCoercive_energyIntegrand` vs public `lemma isCoercive_energyIntegrand_zero_drift` with no attribute; EnergyForm.lean:245,254,263 show the parallel corollaries annotated @[grind =>].
findings.fix: Move/add @[grind =>] to the public isCoercive_energyIntegrand_zero_drift so grind can discharge coercivity goals from UniformlyEllipticOn + MassLowerBoundOn, matching the file's pattern.
findings.issue: The public consumer-facing UniformlyEllipticOn.isCoercive_energyIntegrand_zero_drift carries no automation annotation, while the private general-drift isCoercive_energyIntegrand carries @[grind =>]; the sibling corollaries in EnergyForm.lean (norm_energyIntegrand_apply_le, opNorm_energyIntegrand_le, garding_energyIntegrand_self) are all @[grind =>].

summary: The new MassLowerBoundOn predicate now has a complete, annotated characteristic API and the non-zero-drift coercivity is correctly hidden, but a generic helper is over-exposed and the grind annotation is on the private lemma instead of the public consumer-facing one.
